---
title: "Non-Gaussian Response Distributions"
author: "Simon Frost"
format:
  html:
    code-fold: false
    toc: true
  gfm:
    toc: true
  pdf:
    toc: true
engine: julia
---

## Overview

GAMs are not limited to Gaussian responses. By specifying a **family** (distribution) and **link function**, we can model count data, binary outcomes, positive continuous data, and more. The general model is:

$$
g(\mu_i) = \beta_0 + f_1(x_{1i}) + \cdots + f_p(x_{pi}), \quad y_i \sim \text{Family}(\mu_i, \phi)
$$

This vignette demonstrates three common non-Gaussian families:

| Family | Link | Response type | Example |
|--------|------|---------------|---------|
| Poisson | log | Counts | Species counts, event rates |
| Binomial | logit | Binary / proportions | PresenceвЂ“absence, disease status |
| Gamma | inverse (or log) | Positive continuous | Waiting times, claim sizes |

## Setup

::: {.cell execution_count=1}
``` {.julia .cell-code}
using GAM
using CSV
using StatsAPI: residuals, fitted, r2, deviance
using Statistics: mean
using GLM: LogLink, LogitLink

using DataFrames
using Plots
using Distributions
```
:::



## Poisson GAM вЂ” count data

### Simulating count data

We simulate counts from a Poisson distribution with a smooth log-rate:

$$
y_i \sim \text{Poisson}(\mu_i), \quad \log(\mu_i) = 1 + 1.5 \sin(2\pi x_i)
$$

::: {.cell execution_count=1}
``` {.julia .cell-code}
df_pois = CSV.read("data_poisson.csv", DataFrame)
x = df_pois.x
n = nrow(df_pois)
О· = 1.0 .+ 1.5 .* sin.(2ПЂ .* x)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=1}
```
300-element Vector{Float64}:
 1.0022515463804877
 1.013013980830874
 1.014801884538615
 1.0214214724578388
 1.037208399572455
 1.0690982444331225
 1.0742815178198883
 1.165054158814009
 1.1757202903086204
 1.2015521628982468
 в‹®
 0.6508219129798084
 0.69797588899615
 0.7278818802184517
 0.7517667995108033
 0.7954284540183503
 0.8368919812276477
 0.8383703243966152
 0.8953919908689525
 0.9675123075811386
```
:::
:::



### Fitting the model

::: {.cell execution_count=1}
``` {.julia .cell-code}
m_pois = gam(@formulak(y ~ s(x, k = 15, bs = :cr)), df_pois;
    family = Poisson(), link = LogLink())
m_pois
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=1}
```
Generalized Additive Model

Formula: y ~ 1

Family: Poisson
Link:   LogLink
Method: REML

Parametric coefficients:
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
                Coef.  Std. Error      z  Pr(>|z|)
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
(Intercept)  0.939592   0.0468467  20.06    <1e-88
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ

Approximate significance of smooth terms:
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
Smooth                    edf   Ref.df
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
s(x,bs=cr)               8.00       14
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ

RВІ (adj) = 0.764   Deviance explained = 76.4%
n = 300
```
:::
:::



### Smooth estimate

::: {.cell execution_count=1}
``` {.julia .cell-code}
se_pois = smooth_estimates(m_pois; n = 200)
x_grid = se_pois.covariates[:x]

p1 = plot(x_grid, se_pois.estimate;
    ribbon = 2 .* se_pois.se,
    fillalpha = 0.2,
    label = "s(x) В± 2SE",
    linewidth = 2,
    xlabel = "x",
    ylabel = "f(x) [log scale]",
    title = "Poisson GAM вЂ” smooth on link scale")
plot!(p1, x, О· .- mean(О·);
    label = "true f(x)",
    linestyle = :dash,
    linewidth = 2,
    color = :red)
p1
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=1}
![](04_families_files/figure-commonmark/cell-5-output-1.svg){}
:::
:::



### Deviance residuals

::: {.cell execution_count=1}
``` {.julia .cell-code}
resid_pois = residuals(m_pois; type = :deviance)

p2 = scatter(fitted(m_pois), resid_pois;
    xlabel = "Fitted values",
    ylabel = "Deviance residuals",
    title = "Poisson GAM вЂ” residuals",
    alpha = 0.4,
    markersize = 3,
    label = false)
hline!(p2, [0]; linestyle = :dash, color = :gray, label = false)
p2
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=1}
![](04_families_files/figure-commonmark/cell-6-output-1.svg){}
:::
:::



## Binomial GAM вЂ” binary data

### Simulating binary data

We simulate binary outcomes from a logistic model:

$$
y_i \sim \text{Bernoulli}(p_i), \quad \text{logit}(p_i) = -0.5 + 2\sin(2\pi x_i)
$$

::: {.cell execution_count=1}
``` {.julia .cell-code}
df_bin = CSV.read("data_binomial.csv", DataFrame)
x_bin = df_bin.x
О·_bin = -0.5 .+ 2.0 .* sin.(2ПЂ .* x_bin)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=1}
```
300-element Vector{Float64}:

 -0.38854578391368955
 -0.373831674500529
 -0.3621071915006612
 -0.3019464421915505
 -0.268943862845824

 -0.7139247353867896
 -0.7050111267006494
 -0.5752349322611175
 -0.5557362670383619
 -0.5496105296196276
 -0.5427940453659735
```
:::
:::



### Fitting the model

::: {.cell execution_count=1}
``` {.julia .cell-code}
m_bin = gam(@formulak(y ~ s(x, k = 15, bs = :cr)), df_bin;
    family = Binomial(), link = LogitLink())
m_bin
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=1}

```
Family: Binomial

Parametric coefficients:

                 Coef.  Std. Error      z  Pr(>|z|)
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
(Intercept)  -0.858846    0.159901  -5.37    <1e-07
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ

Approximate significance of smooth terms:

Smooth                    edf   Ref.df
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
s(x,bs=cr)               5.03       14
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ

RВІ (adj) = 0.295   Deviance explained = 25.5%
n = 300
```
:::
:::



### Smooth estimate

::: {.cell execution_count=1}
``` {.julia .cell-code}
se_bin = smooth_estimates(m_bin; n = 200)
x_grid_bin = se_bin.covariates[:x]

p3 = plot(x_grid_bin, se_bin.estimate;
    ribbon = 2 .* se_bin.se,
    fillalpha = 0.2,
    label = "s(x) В± 2SE",
    linewidth = 2,
    xlabel = "x",
    ylabel = "f(x) [logit scale]",
    title = "Binomial GAM вЂ” smooth on link scale")
plot!(p3, x_bin, О·_bin .- mean(О·_bin);
    label = "true f(x)",
    linestyle = :dash,
    linewidth = 2,
    color = :red)
p3
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=1}
![](04_families_files/figure-commonmark/cell-9-output-1.svg){}
:::
:::



### Deviance residuals

::: {.cell execution_count=1}
``` {.julia .cell-code}
resid_bin = residuals(m_bin; type = :deviance)

p4 = scatter(fitted(m_bin), resid_bin;
    xlabel = "Fitted values (probability)",
    ylabel = "Deviance residuals",
    title = "Binomial GAM вЂ” residuals",
    alpha = 0.4,
    markersize = 3,
    label = false)
hline!(p4, [0]; linestyle = :dash, color = :gray, label = false)
p4
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=1}
![](04_families_files/figure-commonmark/cell-10-output-1.svg){}
:::
:::



## Gamma GAM вЂ” positive continuous data

### Simulating positive continuous data

We simulate positive continuous data from a Gamma distribution with a smooth log-mean:

$$
y_i \sim \text{Gamma}(\text{shape}, \text{scale}_i), \quad \log(\mu_i) = 1 + \sin(2\pi x_i)
$$

::: {.cell execution_count=1}
``` {.julia .cell-code}
df_gam = CSV.read("data_gamma.csv", DataFrame)
x_gam = df_gam.x
О·_gam = 1.0 .+ sin.(2ПЂ .* x_gam)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=1}
```
300-element Vector{Float64}:
 1.000850704315502
 1.0039634355393983
 1.0264783803611617
 1.0383131142818598
 1.0751167938723143
 1.0766376001001923
 1.08050888736706
 1.157843246765896
 1.20089524015795
 1.2452611220182526
 в‹®
 0.8188335469516427
 0.819999062363841
 0.8356988327736081
 0.8772715675927307
 0.8829807993731171
 0.9580649708004427
 0.9693231877198806
 0.9749523420975444
 0.9923732280689034
```
:::
:::



### Fitting the model

We use `Gamma()` with `LogLink()` (log link is more commonly used in practice than the canonical inverse link):

::: {.cell execution_count=1}
``` {.julia .cell-code}
m_gam = gam(@formulak(y ~ s(x, k = 15, bs = :cr)), df_gam;
    family = Gamma(), link = LogLink())
m_gam
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=1}
```
Generalized Additive Model

Formula: y ~ 1

Family: Gamma
Link:   LogLink
Method: REML

Parametric coefficients:
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
               Coef.  Std. Error      t  Pr(>|t|)
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
(Intercept)  1.02611    0.024569  41.76    <1e-99
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ

Approximate significance of smooth terms:
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
Smooth                    edf   Ref.df
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
s(x,bs=cr)               6.23       14
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ

RВІ (adj) = 0.362   Deviance explained = 41.6%
Scale est. = 0.1811   n = 300
```
:::
:::



### Smooth estimate

::: {.cell execution_count=1}
``` {.julia .cell-code}
se_gam = smooth_estimates(m_gam; n = 200)
x_grid_gam = se_gam.covariates[:x]

p5 = plot(x_grid_gam, se_gam.estimate;
    ribbon = 2 .* se_gam.se,
    fillalpha = 0.2,
    label = "s(x) В± 2SE",
    linewidth = 2,
    xlabel = "x",
    ylabel = "f(x) [log scale]",
    title = "Gamma GAM вЂ” smooth on link scale")
plot!(p5, x_gam, О·_gam .- mean(О·_gam);
    label = "true f(x)",
    linestyle = :dash,
    linewidth = 2,
    color = :red)
p5
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=1}
![](04_families_files/figure-commonmark/cell-13-output-1.svg){}
:::
:::



### Deviance residuals

::: {.cell execution_count=1}
``` {.julia .cell-code}
resid_gam = residuals(m_gam; type = :deviance)

p6 = scatter(fitted(m_gam), resid_gam;
    xlabel = "Fitted values",
    ylabel = "Deviance residuals",
    title = "Gamma GAM вЂ” residuals",
    alpha = 0.4,
    markersize = 3,
    label = false)
hline!(p6, [0]; linestyle = :dash, color = :gray, label = false)
p6
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=1}
![](04_families_files/figure-commonmark/cell-14-output-1.svg){}
:::
:::



## Comparison

::: {.cell execution_count=1}
``` {.julia .cell-code}
println("Family        EDF      Dev.Expl(%)   Scale")
println("в”Ђ" ^ 55)
for (name, m) in [("Poisson", m_pois), ("Binomial", m_bin), ("Gamma", m_gam)]
    e = round(edf(m)[1]; digits = 2)
    de = round(r2(m) * 100; digits = 1)
    sc = round(m.scale; digits = 4)
    println("$(rpad(name, 14))$(lpad(string(e), 6))  $(lpad(string(de), 12))  $(lpad(string(sc), 8))")
end
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}
```
Family        EDF      Dev.Expl(%)   Scale
```

## Summary

In this vignette we:

1. Simulated count data and fitted a **Poisson GAM** with a log link
2. Simulated binary data and fitted a **Binomial GAM** with a logit link
3. Simulated positive continuous data and fitted a **Gamma GAM** with a log link
4. Examined smooth estimates and deviance residuals for each family
5. Compared EDF and deviance explained across families

Each family uses a different link function to map the linear predictor to the mean of the response distribution, but the smooth estimation machinery is the same.
